Main theorem / novelty tier:novelty, Conjecture 1:novelty, Related work and positioning:novelty

structure.notes: The note preserves the partial-identification locked tuple, includes the required Stage 0 sections, labels assumptions, separates the target ATE from the bounding functional, and gives an actionable math-object checklist.

novelty.notes: The current derivation is a useful sharp finite-envelope reduction, but it does not meet novelty_target=flagship: the admitted unproved five-family facet taxonomy and strict-shrinkage frontier are precisely the more ambitious flagship-level claims, and the note does not show that the one-dimensional cap-envelope opens a previously unresolved regime relative to Balke--Pearl, Richardson--Robins, or automated discrete-bound methods.

correctness.notes: The principal-strata parametrization, fixed-t interval reduction, endpoint algebra, compactness/sharpness argument, face-limit proposition, and numerical minimality example are mathematically coherent under the stated assumptions.

perItemFindings.label: Main theorem / novelty tier
perItemFindings.one_line: Assessed tier is subfield below novelty_target=flagship; the theorem proves an explicit reduction of an established finite LP with two mass caps, but not a regime-opening flagship contribution.

perItemFindings.label: Conjecture 1
perItemFindings.one_line: The note explicitly only partially confirms the proposed nonredundant five-family facet taxonomy and strict-shrinkage catalogue, so it has not proved every flagship conjectural component.

perItemFindings.label: Related work and positioning
perItemFindings.one_line: The comparison explains that the result differs from Balke--Pearl and fixed-defier sensitivity work, but it does not identify a published open problem or prove a strict extension to a wider class as required for flagship tier.

review.verbatim_critique: Structure passes: the artifact has the required sections, the locked parameter block correctly identifies a PartialID anchor with observed distribution P_{Y,D,Z}, target theta_ATE, IV+BDN restrictions, and a bounding functional, and Section 14 separates probabilistic objects from deterministic algebraic objects. Correctness also passes on the current theorem: solving the first-stage equations by t=pi_F gives pi_A=q_{01}-t, pi_N=q_{10}-t, pi_C=q_{00}-q_{10}+t; the x and w interval constraints in Section 7 exactly encode support for the observed outcome equations; substituting into theta_ATE gives R-x+w+y_{1N}-y_{0A}; and the lower and upper fixed-t choices produce B_L(t)=R-U_x(t)+L_w(t)-a(t) and B_U(t)=R-L_x(t)+U_w(t)+n(t). The compact-polytope and piecewise-affine knot arguments in Section 11 are sufficient for sharpness and computability, and the Section 13 numerical example correctly shows nonredundancy of the never-taker cap. The revision is therefore not a mathematical repair of the proved envelope, but a novelty-floor repair. The orchestrator requires flagship. The current theorem is an explicit one-dimensional evaluation of a binary-IV partial-identification LP with two additional mass caps. That is useful, but the note itself says Conjecture 1 is only partially confirmed as to the nonredundant five-family taxonomy and strict-shrinkage catalogue, and it replaces the proposed closed-form facet catalogue by an active-knot enumeration. It also does not show that Balke--Pearl, Richardson--Robins, symbolic-bound methods, or monotonicity-violation sensitivity papers left this simultaneous defier/never-taker-cap regime open in the flagship sense. To meet the floor, Stage 0 would need to prove the stronger finite affine facet catalogue and the necessary-and-sufficient strict-shrinkage frontier, or otherwise position and prove the result as a genuine regime-opening theorem against a named published gap. Without that upgrade, the current derivation should be treated as subfield-tier rather than accepted under a flagship target.

report_summary: Related work and positioning:novelty, Theorem 1 / Conjecture confirmation:correctness, Boundary-distribution sharpness certificate:correctness, Strict-shrinkage frontier:novelty

structure.notes: The note preserves the partial-identification locked tuple, required Stage 0 sections, labelled assumptions, cluster-aware bounding-functional role in Section 7, and an actionable math-objects checklist.

novelty.notes: The current derivation is below the orchestrator's flagship floor: it gives a useful finite-dimensional LP reduction and cap-sensitive IV envelope, but does not establish a flagship regime-opening result relative to Balke--Pearl, Richardson--Robins, or general discrete symbolic-bound methods.

correctness.notes: The scalar reduction and endpoint algebra appear basically correct, but the strongest theorem claims about five nonredundant facet families and boundary certificates are asserted through generic LP-duality language rather than actually exhibiting the promised family-level certificates.

perItemFindings.label: Related work and positioning
perItemFindings.one_line: tier=subfield below novelty_target=flagship; the note does not show that the simultaneous defier-cap/never-taker-cap envelope resolves a published open regime rather than specializing known finite discrete-bound LP machinery.

perItemFindings.label: Theorem 1 / Conjecture confirmation
perItemFindings.one_line: The proof confirms sharp endpoints via scalar reduction, but the claim that every pruned dual basis partitions into five nonredundant families lacks explicit exposed bases or observable open-region witnesses.

perItemFindings.label: Boundary-distribution sharpness certificate
perItemFindings.one_line: The promised constructive maps Gamma_f are described abstractly by complementary slackness, but the note does not give the facet-by-facet constructions needed to cover degenerate zero-cell and cap-binding cases.

perItemFindings.label: Strict-shrinkage frontier
perItemFindings.one_line: The Boolean frontier is finite and computable only through abstract extreme-point catalogues; this is useful but not the explicit new mathematical object needed for a flagship-tier derivation.

review.verbatim_critique: The partial-identification structure is usable: the locked tuple matches qid=pid_iv_bounded_defier_envelope, specialization=v1, observable law P_{Y,D,Z}, ATE target, IV+BDN restrictions, and the proposed bounding functional. Sections 3--7 correctly separate the target ATE from the bounding functional, and the labelled assumptions are adequate for the scalar reduction. The core algebra in Sections 7 and 11 appears sound: first-stage equations leave t=pi_F, the caps imply t in [max{0,q10-bar_nu,q10-q00}, min{q01,q10,bar_delta}], the x and w intervals correctly encode the four outcome equations, and B_L(t), B_U(t) correctly minimize and maximize R-x+w+y1N-y0A at fixed t. The counterexample in Section 13 also checks numerically. However, the main theorem overclaims relative to what is proved. The sharp endpoint interval follows, but the confirmation of Conjecture 1's five nonredundant affine-facet families is only asserted via generic support-of-dual-basis language. To make that part correct, Stage 0 must either enumerate representative exposed bases/open regions for Balke--Pearl, defier-cap, never-taker-cap, joint-cap, and support-fill-in families, or weaken the theorem to the scalar sharp-envelope result plus an algorithmic extreme-point catalogue. Similarly, Conjecture 2 asks for constructive boundary maps Gamma_f covering zero observed cells and cap-binding faces; the proof says complementary slackness gives such maps but does not spell out the case handling or feasibility regions. The larger blocking issue is novelty under the enforced flagship floor. The derivation is a useful hand reduction of a binary-IV finite LP with two principal-stratum mass caps, but the note does not establish that this opens a previously unresolved regime, strictly extends a named published theorem to a class explicitly left open, or gives a flagship-level equivalence/non-equivalence frontier. General symbolic and automated discrete-bound methods are cited but not overcome; the result is positioned as a clearer and more auditable specialization rather than a flagship mathematical advance. Therefore the artifact cannot be accepted under novelty_target=flagship.

reason: Case 6b (unsalvageable conjecture at flagship floor) + regression pattern (a) — both reviewer rounds reject on the same novelty-tier defect: the proposal as conjectured reduces to a useful subfield-tier finite-LP specialization with two mass caps, and the stronger five-family facet catalogue / strict-shrinkage frontier that would close the flagship gap was first only partially confirmed and then overclaimed via generic LP-duality language. The reviewer is explicit that flagship requires either a constructive exposed-basis enumeration (which Codex tried and failed to produce honestly) OR positioning against a named published open regime (which the conjecture's framing against Balke–Pearl / Richardson–Robins / symbolic discrete-bound methods does not deliver). Re-deriving the same conjecture at Stage 0 would burn budget on the same novelty-floor defect, not a fixable derivation step.
proposed_action: Pivot the angle: abandon the simultaneous defier-cap/never-taker-cap envelope framing (currently a one-dimensional LP reduction with two mass caps, tier=subfield) and propose a new angle that either (i) names a specific published open regime in the Balke–Pearl / Richardson–Robins / Manski-IV literature that the new angle strictly resolves, or (ii) targets a regime-opening equivalence/non-equivalence frontier rather than a finite-facet sharpness statement. Stage -1 should explicitly avoid the active-knot / dual-basis enumeration framing that just failed.
